TextENGNew: "Now then! And so—!! It's finally lunchtime!!"
Mion gleefully declared the start of lunch.
"Oh-ho-ho-ho! It is finally lunchtime~! One of us here will have to do the punishment game, yes?"
"Hau... The punishment game this time... is a bit scary..."
"I'm sorry, Rena went totally all-out making hers...!"
Even without going all-out, I didn't think Rena had any chance of losing today's contest...
"...Mii. We tried our best as well, each in our own way."
changeReason: New translation corrects 'Meep' to 'Mii' (Rika's actual verbal tic) and adds 'each in our own way' matching それぞれに from the Japanese

TextENGNew: "Did Satoko and Rika-chan make separate bentos today? You two usually make the same thing, but that wouldn't work for a proper showdown."
"Yes. It is perfectly fine. Both Rika and I made sure to prepare our own separately."
"Wow, Satoko-chan and Rika-chan both tried so hard! How exciting!"
"...What I didn't quite expect was that Kei-chan actually managed to bring a proper bento! Good for you, Kei-chan! Looks like you really can do it if you put your mind to it!"
I scratched my head with a wry smile.
...Today's bento had been made entirely by Satoko last night.
There was not a single touch of my own creativity in it. So there was really nothing for them to praise me about.
changeReason: Corrects 'personality' to 'creativity' matching オリジナリティ (originality)

TextENGNew: "I wonder what Keiichi-kun's bento looks like! Hau, I can't wait!"
"Don't expect too much... It's just last night's leftovers packed in a box..."
"...Leftovers from supper are the foundation of a good bento, you know."
"Oh-ho-ho-ho! That means we will also learn what Keiichi-san had for dinner last night—two birds with one stone!"
...Was it really better for me to keep quiet about Satoko coming over to cook last night...?
Of course it was better to keep quiet.
...If it came out that this bento wasn't something I made myself, I'd be guaranteed to lose the punishment game on the spot.
Today's punishment game... also known as saying something bad about curry right in front of Chie-sensei, the "Curry Fighter"!
changeReason: Changes 'curry maniac' to the literal nickname 'Curry Fighter' matching 別名カレーファイター

TextENGNew: It wasn't all that different from telling someone to jump from the roof of a hundred-story building.
changeReason: Corrects 'hundredth-story window' to 'roof of a hundred-story building' matching 屋上 (rooftop)

TextENGNew: ...Which is exactly the same as telling someone to die...!
"But Mii-chan... How are we actually going to decide who wins and loses today? Keiichi-kun made his bento too, so maybe we should just call it a draw today?"
"...That is a fair point. Yesterday's punishment game was more like a prod to get Keiichi-san cooking for himself in the first place."
"...Keiichi made his own too, so it is a draw."
Everyone else had made their bentos to win. Mine was just leftovers stuffed into a box. There was never any contest to begin with...
I could tell clearly that Satoko and Rika-chan were trying to end the showdown peacefully out of consideration for me.
...But! There was no way Club President Mion Sonozaki would let it end there...!!

"What kind of sweet talk is that! An unresolved, grey-area showdown is absolutely out of the question!! When two sides clash, there must be a decisive outcome! Making up and calling it a draw—do you think we're playing around here?!"
changeReason: Corrects 'optimistic' to 'sweet talk' better matching 甘っちょろい (naive/soft)

TextENGNew: "I-I knew you'd say that... There's no way someone with your competitive streak would let it end like that..."
This was bad...
...At this rate... I was certain to be the sole loser.
...But, well... I didn't actually make this bento...
...Maybe... I had to accept my loss and resign myself to it...
"...Then, Mii. How do you propose we decide who wins?"
"Grading each other's bentos wouldn't be fair."
"How about an open judging! Hey, everyone!! Gather round~!!"
Mion called out to everyone with her club-president voice, though people had only just started eating.
"I'd like all of you to judge five bentos!! Write your top three on a piece of paper and submit it! Understood?!"
Everyone in the class was used to being swept up in Mion's club activities. A chorus of enthusiastic voices rose up from all around.
...Everyone already knew about the fatally severe punishment game awaiting the loser in today's contest... and they were all desperately curious to see how it would end for the unlucky one.
...In the sense of wanting to watch something terrifying, of course!
"All right... Ready, everyone?! I'll go first, shall I?! Here goes!!"
Mion threw open the lid of her lavish, lacquered bento box with a great flourish.
A cheer erupted at the sight of the brilliant spread inside...!!
"Ho—how amazing!! It looks just like New Year's food?!"

"It can't be... It has to be a lie. She must have transferred the contents from some catering service. Such completely authentic Japanese cooking—Mion! Mion!! There's no way the Mion Sonozaki I know could have made this!!!"
"Mm... Mii-chan could make something like this if she got serious... Though she almost never does..."
"Khu-khu-khu...! And just who do you think this old man is? Given enough time and ingredients, I could reproduce the Manchu Han Imperial Feast—khu-khu-khu!"
An audible swallow ran through the entire classroom.
...And then everyone simultaneously began writing scores on their notepads.
Of course they did. A perfect score just for the visual impact alone...!
"All right, next up is Rena's bento! Rena is quite a talented cook too!"
"...Rena's bento is always the centerpiece of any table."
"Ahahahahaha! Thank you all! Then Rena will reveal hers now. Ta-dah☆"
Rena's bento was...
...a gorgeous two-toned spread of golden egg and seasoned minced meat—another classic, the soboro bento!
The side dishes had been prepared with great care, and there were plenty of them.
...She had put in more effort than usual, yet it was all done with a quiet nonchalance—nothing showy about it.
One couldn't overlook the homely warmth it had, something Mion's bento lacked.
"...As expected of you...! This... should score quite highly!"
"...Indeed. Even judging strictly, there is nothing to deduct points for. ...Impressive as ever!"
changeReason: Corrects 'It may look rigid' to 'Even judging strictly' matching 厳しめに見ても (even when viewed strictly)

TextENGNew: Once again, every one of the judges simultaneously wrote their scores on their notepads.
Some seemed to be revising their opinions compared to the score they gave Mion.
...It looked like the whole classroom was splitting into a Mion faction and a Rena faction.
On every face, it was plain as day—everyone wanted to be tasting them, not grading them!
"...Then, it is our turn next."
"Yes! Then, we shall reveal ours!!"
Come to think of it...
Satoko and Rika-chan had come over and eaten with me at my house last night...
It was rather impressive that they had still managed to prepare bentos...
The contents of their two bento boxes... were clearly inferior when compared to Mion's artistry and Rena's perfection.
...But precisely because of that, you could feel how hard they had tried.
The slightly rough way the toppings were arranged gave the impression they had done their absolute best... which made you feel a kind of warm bias, wanting to give them extra points.
A few people touched by that very thing once again revised their scores and wrote them down on their notepads.
"...Satoko, Rika-chan. You both still managed to prepare bentos for today... The result might not match Rena's or Mion's, but given what you went through, that's pretty incredible..."
Satoko and Rika-chan looked at each other and broke into wide, satisfied grins.
"This is... a secret, yes...? Mumble mumble."
Satoko whispered the truth in my ear. I slipped away from the group... and quietly searched for my classmates' bento boxes.
...There they were—Tomita-kun's and Okamura-kun's bento boxes!
I peered carefully inside, and...
Whoa!!
Half the contents were gone!!!  N-Not bad!!
So in the end, Satoko and Rika-chan hadn't made bentos for today... so they pulled off the bold move of getting Tomita-kun and Okamura-kun to share their bentos with them!!

"...Hey, Tomita-kun, Okamura-kun.
How did they buy you off?"
"W-W-W-W-W-What are you on about so suddenly, Maebara-san?! W-W-W-W-When did we ever get bought off, or for what?!"
"T-T-T-That's right...! It's not like Satoko and them got us to give up half our bentos just for taking over our plant-watering duty...!!"
...Curse you, Satoko.
To think she'd start pulling tricks like that...!
Satoko grinned triumphantly, flashing her little fangs.
"Oh my, this is a trick I learned from you, Keiichi-san? Oh-ho-ho-ho!"
"...Oh-ho-ho, indeed."
Tsk, tsk—how shallow of them, getting bought off with just watering duty...
Listen here—if you want to buy a man off, you need something with far more heat than that!!
For example... hmm,

...Three tickets entitling the bearer to walk Satoko and Rika-chan on all fours with tails and leashes for the whole day or something.
...Whoaaaa—w-would that... would that actually get me bought off too?!?!

...I was deep in that fantasy when Mion clapped me on the shoulder and dragged me back to reality.
(Dammit, just when it was getting good...!)
"Now then!! Next up is Kei-chan's bento!! We were all expecting great things after yesterday's declaration of his... so how did it actually turn out?!"
"Keiichi-kun can really deliver when it counts! Rena was really looking forward to today. I really was!"
...Urgh, the expectations...
But in the end... it was just last night's dinner packed in a box.
And it wasn't even something I'd cooked.
...How was something like that supposed to hold up against Mion's and Rena's bentos...?
"Oh, to hell with it!! Take a look and try not to faint!! Heeeere!!"
I flung the lid off the bento box.

...There wasn't much in the way of cheering from the crowd.
...Well, I supposed there wouldn't be...
"Ahahaha! Keiichi-kun, it's plain how hard you tried! Rena can tell, you know."

"Rena...
You don't have to force a compliment..."

"Umm... How does it look?
Would you say Keiichi-san actually gave it a real effort...?"

"Khu-khu-khu-khu! Well, I want to give credit where credit is due... but a showdown has no mercy.
Khu-khu-khu-khu!"
Mion's cold, mocking laugh...! It seemed to speak for everyone's verdict, just as it was.

"Umm... Hmm...
Keiichi-kun, this is actually quite good for a first attempt, don't you think?
I think everyone should give it a little higher score..."
"...But a showdown shows no mercy."

"Khu-khu-khu-khu...
Now then, Kei-chan.
If you have any final words before we tally the results, I'll hear them now.
Khu-khu-khu!"
"W-What do you mean, final words?"
"I mean that a well-placed appeal to the audience might get you a few bonus points."

"...Khu-khu-khu! After all, all you need to avoid in today's contest is last place.
With a little luck... you might manage to escape the punishment game, you know?"
"Oh-ho-ho-ho! That's the same as saying outright that Keiichi-san's bento is going to come in last, isn't it?"

"Now, this old man never said anyone's bento was going to be last or anything of the sort.
Khu-khu-khu!"
Mion let out a repulsive, crowing laugh.
...By contrast, the smile on Satoko's face was growing awkward.
...Right.

This bento was packed with last night's side dishes.
...Which meant it was essentially a bento that Satoko had made.
And Mion had just bluntly declared it would come in last place...

"Mii-chan, that's a little mean, isn't it?
Poor Keiichi-kun."

"Rena... Kei-chan knew he couldn't afford to lose today's bento showdown, right?
He went into it with that full declaration yesterday, knowing all that.
I don't think we need to go easy on him at all, do you?"
"Right, Satoko?"

"Y-Yes! That is exactly so! And to think he shows up like this—it makes me want to laugh!
Ohh-ho-ho-ho-ho!"
"Hey, wait, Satoko-chan! You can't just laugh at him."
"But how could one not laugh at such a bento—it is simply hilarious!"
"This looks like something bought from the prepared foods section of a shop—honestly, the disappointment is something else entirely!"

"Just look—this one was clearly pried out of a tin, wasn't it? And this cucumber here—the knife didn't go all the way through, it's not even cut cleanly~. Kei-chan, you've never used a knife before, have you. Your inexperience is obvious."
"...Yes, yes, inexperience would be putting it mildly—!"

"The color scheme is quite monotonous as well. I'm almost impressed by the sheer lack of taste!
Ah-ha-ha-ha!"

"Urgh... W-Well, this was literally my first time packing anything into a bento box...
Of course it was going to look a little rough around the edges..."

"Kei-chan, Kei-chan.
The way you packed it is the least of the problems!
Tomita, Okamura.
Give us a proper breakdown~"
The arrows landed on our underclassmen Tomita-kun and Okamura-kun.
Tomita-kun pushed up his glasses with a firm click and stepped forward.

"The fatal flaw in Maebara-san's bento.
It is the color palette.
As you know, all color ultimately rests on what are called the three primary colors.
In other words, if you want a well-balanced arrangement, you need at minimum three colors, even excluding white and black."

"Indeed. However.
If you remove the white and black from Maebara-san's bento, all that remains is a murky dark green."
A-Ah, murky dark green...
What a thoroughly unappetizing way to describe it...
...I had forgotten.
Tomita-kun and Okamura-kun had been bought off by Rika-chan and the others—so they were doing everything in their power to put me in last place and let them win.
...Gah, they were bold about it.
It was almost endearing, how thoroughly they were carrying out the work they'd been paid for.
"And the side dishes are joyless too—all pre-made stuff and tinned food."
"In short, of the three essential elements of any bento— love, heart, and taste—we can confidently say Maebara-san's bento is missing two of the three!!"
changeReason: Changes 'spirit' to 'heart' throughout the courtroom scene, better matching 気持ち (feeling/heart)

TextENGNew: The classroom, half-convinced by the impenetrably convoluted explanation, let out a collective murmur of vague admiration.

"...Khu-khu-khu! No love and no heart.
Case closed, isn't it?
Keiichi Maebara!!"
"This sounds a little harsh... But if it helped you realize how much fun cooking can be, Keiichi-kun... maybe that's enough? Maybe?"
"...More than anything, I'm simply relieved it's not me doing the punishment game."
Rena gave a helpless little "ehehe♪" of a smile.
"Oh-ho-ho-ho!! It is settled, Keiichi-san! You are the one who will be doing the punishment game!! Ohh-ho-ho-ho-ho...!"
She laughed the laugh of someone delighting in another's misfortune... but she didn't look like she meant it.
I knew that the bento she was publicly ridiculing was practically her own handiwork—and I could clearly tell just how painful that laughter really was.
...A moment ago, Okamura-kun had said this bento had no heart.
...But among all the bentos laid out here today, there was none that had more heart than this one.
This meal she had made for me yesterday... with such genuine good cheer (even though her face had been quite surly about it)... I would not stand by and let it end in mockery...!
"Now then! Last place, Keiichi-san?! Why don't you quietly admit your honest defeat already!!"
"...If I could ask you to hold on just a moment."
When I spoke in a low, measured voice, the expressions of the club members around me shifted.
Mion clicked her tongue.
...So this man bares his fangs again at the very last moment...!

"My bento was defeated for lacking heart.
That was the explanation you gave.
Am I correct, Okamura-kun?"
Okamura-kun, suddenly placed in the spotlight, looked momentarily flustered—but replied almost immediately, stammering, that yes, I was right.

"What defines heart? Hardship?
Effort?
Sweat and tears?
Their crystallization?!
Then what score do you give to these burns that still mark my hands?!?!"
"W-Wow... Keiichi-kun, those burns... Did hot oil splatter on you...?!"
The burn marks from that near-disaster the day before looked perfectly like the scars of someone who had struggled mightily in the kitchen. Perfect.
I could plainly see that the classroom had no choice but to acknowledge the effort I had put in!

"...I see.
...Turning one's failures in the kitchen into evidence of hard-won experience...
As I thought... this man cannot be underestimated!"

"That's right. I lack skill. I am so incompetent in the kitchen it makes yesterday's grandstanding seem pathetic. But I did not run away! I fought!! And I produced these results!! Is that not heart?! Well? Is it or isn't it?!"
"Rena wants to acknowledge it...! If the spirit to challenge something isn't recognized... nobody can ever move forward!"
The girls in the class began to nod along.
...Yes! The wind is in my favor!!
But Tomita-kun and Okamura-kun—bought off by the girls they were hopelessly in love with—weren't about to lose either.
They shifted into the counterattack, clearly going above and beyond what they'd been paid for!!
"I understand! I understand what you're feeling, Maebara-san! However—if heart alone decided cooking, there would be no need for professional chefs in this world!"
"Exactly. If a chef brought you a burnt black hamburger steak and said they put their heart into it and tried their hardest—would you pay for that?"
changeReason: Adds 'hamburger' to 'steak' and 'black' to 'burnt', matching 黒焦げのハンバーグステーキ (Japanese hamburger steak dish)

TextENGNew: "You wouldn't, would you?!"
"That is exactly what this is!"
That's right, that's right, exactly!
The male students rallied behind Team Tomita-Okamura.
...Gah, they did have a point.
"But what about consideration?! Even if the steak came out burnt black!! If I knew how hard the mother who made it had worked—I would eat it!! I would eat every last bite!!"
Clap clap clap clap!!

This time, the girls in the class took my side.
...Good!
Now we're evenly matched!!
"That said, a bento is fundamentally love given the form of a meal!!"

"And it only carries meaning when it reaches the heart of the recipient.
Does your bento reach us in that way, Maebara-san?!
No—it does not reach me!"
""That's awful!!!""
The girls in the class erupted in a chorus of boos at that remark! Even Okamura-kun looked flustered.
"Ah—order, order! Spectators, please refrain from making comments!"
"Your Honor! I would like to call upon the testimony of Ms. Rena Ryuugu, a leading authority in the culinary arts, to speak to how much heart this bento contains!"
"Huh, um...!"

"R-Rena thinks this bento has plenty of heart!
There may be plenty of rough spots, it's true..."
"But cooking isn't only about what shows on the surface!"
Once again, an eruption of applause and cheering from the girls!
And the boys responded with a chorus of boos!!

"Y-Your Honor! The opinions of Maebara-san and Ryuugu-san are highly emotional and far removed from common sense.
They are inappropriate as evidence!!"
"Order, order!!@k@vS19/03/500300332. The prosecution's rebuttal is permitted.@k@vS19/03/500300333. Go ahead!"
changeReason: Old says 'objection is sustained' while new says 'rebuttal is permitted'; the legal terminology differs but both allow the prosecution to continue speaking.

TextENGNew:
"Ahem.
Let us substitute the bento in this case with a birthday present, for the sake of argument."

"Let us say that on my birthday, I—Tomita—received a hand-knitted sweater.
And that he—Okamura—received a ten-thousand-yen gift certificate for a bookshop!"

...That was a strangely specific example.
Was it actually just a hypothetical?

"By any reasonable standard, it is obvious which of the two carries more heart.
The hand-knitted sweater, of course!"
"In the language of bentos, this would be the equivalent of a homemade side dish!"
"And the gift certificate—a mere voucher for money—is the equivalent of something purchased, that is to say, the cold and soulless tinned food!!"
Murmur murmur murmur murmur!!
The boys and girls all began arguing amongst themselves about presents.
They were all talking about what gifts they had received in the past...!
Damn...!
Switching to a topic that could easily capture everyone's imagination... Tomita-kun, well played!!
If I let this go on, I'll lose the initiative!!
"Objection!! This matter concerns the heart within a bento! It is an entirely different matter from birthday presents, which are a special circumstance!!"

"Overruled.
Will the prosecution please continue?"
D-Dammit!!

"Now then.
What is important here is that the heart of the giver and the heart of the receiver are entirely different."

"Before you now are a hand-knitted sweater that is a little uneven on both sides, and a ten-thousand-yen gift certificate for a bookshop. If you were asked which you would prefer—which would you take?!
The certificate, yes?!"
"It's ten thousand yen! Ten thousand!!"
"Can you calculate how many volumes of manga you could buy with that?!"
"You could instantly complete an entire run if it were around ten or so volumes!!"
"Dozens and dozens of delightful, entertaining manga...!!"
"The bliss that would bring to your heart cannot be compared to a sweater!"
The boys gave unanimous applause at that.

...Materialistic bunch...
...Hmph, but that was just how boys that age were.
I'd probably choose the gift certificate over the sweater myself, honestly...
Hey, weren't you two the ones who said it was all about love, heart, and taste just a moment ago?!
When did it quietly become "taste trumps all"?! That's where they'd landed now!!

"In conclusion, I believe it has been demonstrated that there is no causal relationship between the heart of the giver and the heart of the receiver.
...That is to say!! No matter how much suffering went into Maebara-san's bento!"
"That suffering cannot become a reason for any leniency in our scoring!!"
"T-That is sophistry, Tomita-kun!! The heart is unquestionably there! Whether it reaches the recipient or not is the recipient's own problem!!"
""That's right!!""
""Boooo!!!""
""Taste is what matters!!""
""Enemy of women!!""
""What did you say?!""
Ah, the courtroom was now in total chaos...!!
But I could not afford to lose!!
And not only to avoid the punishment game.
But also for Satoko's... honor!!

"Now then.
Defense—do you have any rebuttal?"
Slowly, with quiet force... I nodded, and took the floor.

Tomita-kun. Okamura-kun.
...Did you two truly think you could beat me with words alone...?!?!

"The prosecution just stated that a work should be judged regardless of the heart the maker put into it.
Is that correct?!"
...I had started speaking formally without even noticing...
"Yes, that is correct! No matter how much labor Maebara-san invested in this bento—that labor does not and cannot factor into the scoring!"

"Then allow me to pose a question to the prosecution.
If I, Maebara, were to place the bento I made today beside one purchased from a convenience store...
Would both receive the same score...?!"
The prosecution hesitated.
...Got them!!!
"Exactly!! A convenience store bento does not even merit comparison with a homemade one!"
"No matter how poorly made it may be, a homemade bento contains immeasurably more heart than anything merely purchased ever could!!"
"And that heart is being recognized in the scoring!! Which means the heart the maker put into the bento has already reached its recipients!!"
"Admit it!!!"
Baaaaoom!!
With those impactful words written on the air behind me, I leveled my finger at them!!
The girls—Rena at their head—burst into applause!!
Even some of the boys were starting to come around!
"...Ngggghhhh!! A-Acknowledged..."

"You acknowledge it?! You acknowledge it?! Very well—then let us move on to why a convenience store bento has no heart.
What precisely does heart in a bento even mean?!"
"O-Objection! Maebara-san is attempting to bamboozle the court with abstract expressions!"

"Objection overruled.
This sounds interesting—let him finish."
I had them.
...Now I would show them the difference in our caliber!!

"Your Honor.
I would like to call Ms. Rika Furude to the stand as a witness!"
Even for Rika-chan, that must have come completely out of nowhere.
She could surely never have imagined that the point of my sword would swing her way.

...But Rika-chan.
You said it yourself, didn't you?!

"Ms. Rika Furude.
Your bento today is very well made.
It looks delicious."
Yes.
Satoko's and Rika-chan's bentos were made from half of Tomita-kun's and Okamura-kun's.
The faces of the prosecutors Tomita-kun and Okamura-kun went pale... but it was too late!!

"Rena.
Would you bring Okamura-kun's bento box over here?"

"Hm?
Sure, okay!"
"Y-Your Honor!! My bento box has no relevance to this case!!"

"Objection overruled.
...Khu-khu-khu!"
"Your Honor, I submit Okamura-kun's bento box as evidence! Behold!! The contents of this box!!!"

"Hmm.
...Khu-khu-khu, there's only half left in here...!"
"It isn't about whether there's half—please look more carefully!! There—!!"
""Whaaaat?!?! They're completely identical!!!""
"Your Honor, as you can see! Ms. Rika Furude's bento is stolen from Okamura-kun's! There is no way anything like that could have heart in it!!!!"
"N-No, this is... merely a coincidence...!!"
"Is it not already clear?! Heart—is a game of catch between giver and receiver!! Then the bento box is the ball!"

"And this bento was entrusted from mother to son—from Okamura-kun's mother to him! So the heart packed into this box can only be received by him, Okamura-kun!! Once someone cuts in and takes it away... there is no heart left in it at all!!!"
Dun-dun-duuunnnn!!

"Huh?
What?
I-Is that true?!
Rika-chan, is it true...?!"
"Nipah~♪"

"Khu-khu-khu-khu.
...I see how it is."
Judge Mion folded her arms and began to ruminate...
...The Mion who was laughing like that—there was no guessing what was going through her head.
Plainly speaking, I had no idea whether I would win or lose...!!

"All right.
...Before the verdict—I'll start with the main ruling."

Silence.
The entire classroom went quiet...

"First.
...There is no doubt that, from an objective standpoint, the bento of the accused Keiichi Maebara must be rated as very poor.
This is made clear by the collective scoring results before us!"
...Gah!

"However, as Kei-chan said, cooking is not all about taste.
Heart too should be recognized as a kind of hidden seasoning.
But in terms of scoring, that amounts to a fraction of a point.
It is so subtle that it does not rise to the level of altering the overall verdict."
...Grrk!

"Now then.
I would like to return to the original premise of today's bento showdown.
What was the fundamental condition of today's contest?"

"That's right.
The showdown was held to determine whether the accused, Keiichi Maebara, could cook for himself—and to measure the results of that!"

"Yes.
That's right!
Keiichi-kun made his own!"

...Urk.
That stung my conscience a little... but the showdown was merciless.
"Then the whole premise of this contest was self-sufficiency—namely, whether or not the bento was homemade!!"
"And if Rika-chan failed to meet that premise... that is a problem far more fundamental than how her bento turned out!! In other words... she would be disqualified, wouldn't she?!"
"...Nipah~♪"
Rika-chan gave a nipah~, and she looked as though she had already made her peace with what was coming.

"D-Don't take it personally, Rika-chan.
At the club, things are merciless."

"...Keiichi did well, as expected.
You fought to the very end."
Rika-chan reached up and requested my head, then petted it.
"And with that—case closed!!! Today's last place goes to... Rika Furudeeee!!!"
""Wooooaaahh...!!!""
A miraculous reversal!!!
And last place went to... Rika-chan, the very last person anyone would have expected!!
The whole classroom erupted in noise!!!!

"...H-Hmph.
To think you managed to avoid last place with such a terrible bento."
Satoko looked at me with spite in her eyes, but her voice came out strangely muted.

"There's no way I'd come in last.
Tell me what in this bento deserves to be last."
"B... But! It's all... badly made side dishes and tinned food... and it has no style... and on top of that, and on top of that...!"
Grab!!
Satoko was growing increasingly self-deprecating—so I grabbed her head roughly and stroked it.

"Didn't I say so?! Heart matters.
This bento isn't just appearances—it's full of all sorts of flavors, you know?!"
"But... heart isn't everything...!! The taste is... completely off, and..."

"What's off about it?! I thought it was great! It was absolutely great!! That's why I packed it!! If dinner last night had been terrible, I would never have brought it. I would have come to school with bread and a milk carton!!"
Oh, just be quiet now.
I stroked her head, tilting it back and forth as I did.
Without my noticing, Rena had come up behind us.
She was giggling softly.
...It was the face of someone who had known everything from the very beginning.
"When did you figure out I hadn't made it?"

"When you showed us the burns, I think?
I mean, there's nothing in your bento that needs frying in oil...!"
"...U-Umm, umm...! Rena-san, aren't you under some kind of misunderstanding?! I-I didn't do anything...!"

"Hmm?
Satoko-chan, you didn't make this bento?
Then I suppose Keiichi-kun really did make it himself?
Oh. Well then—I guess he did."
Rena smiled mischievously and turned from Satoko to me.

"When you cut cucumbers, you hold the knife like this and draw it back like so.
As for color, you can express all kinds of variety by incorporating vegetables wisely!"
...That lecture wasn't obviously meant for me.

"But you have gotten a lot better than the last time I saw you.
You've really been studying!
I'm sure at this rate you'll be able to make better and better dishes!"
And she continued directing words clearly meant for Satoko at me instead.
...Satoko wore an uncertain expression, unable even to nod in agreement.

"Anyway, no matter what anyone says! I like today's bento.
I'm not sharing it with anyone today! I'll eat the whole thing myself!!"
"Wow, how stingy...!"
"Rena wants to try some too! Too!"
As we went back and forth like that, Satoko rubbed her eyes as though she were drowsy, then addressed Mion and everyone else.
"Ah, um... I must confess something too! ...I had Tomita-san share his bento with me!"

"Idiot!
...Houjou, why did you say that...?!"

If she had kept quiet, at least she would have been spared...
Tomita-kun looked at her with an expression full of regret and astonishment.

"Huh?
Is that true?!
Really?!
Oh my...☆"
Mion twirled her finger, then pointed at the hallway, sentencing Satoko to the punishment game as well.
Satoko left the classroom behind her with an easy nonchalance, as though she were simply accompanying Rika-chan on an errand.

"...I cannot let Rika go alone.
I will accompany her to the very depths of hell."
"...You need not follow me quite that far."
"Keiichi-san truly is dreadful! To humiliate only Rika like that—what a genuinely spiteful person he is!"
I hadn't meant it that way...
I simply couldn't stand to repay Satoko's kindness with betrayal...

"...Hey?
Satoko really is a bit of a fool, isn't she?"
Rika-chan smiled gently and said it so only I could hear.
"Now then! Let us be off, Rika! Doing the punishment game is one of our duties as club members!!"
Satoko seemed oddly happy—not a single trace of the frustrated look she normally wore when she lost.
...Hmph.

...Rika-chan was right, wasn't she.
Every single one of them—what a bunch of fools!
"Mion! I'll come clean too!! I didn't make my bento today! I was the same! So I'm going with them! To the punishment game!!"
"Huh?! Even you, Kei-chan?! W-What is going on here...?!"

"Uhuhuhu...! Good luck!
With the punishment game!
I'll have the first aid kit ready."

"...Please call an ambulance in advance.
And a hearse too, depending on how it goes..."
I left behind a bewildered Mion and a happily laughing Rena, and stepped out into the hallway.
"...W-What do you think you are doing?! You finally won for once! To voluntarily put yourself forward for the punishment game—you must have rather eccentric tastes!"

"I suppose so.
Eccentric tastes, for sure."

"...Honestly! You are taking today's punishment game far too lightly?! We are one thing, but something dreadful might actually befall you, Keiichi-san! You had better be ready to have one or two ribs broken!"
"U-Urgh... That sounds painful... I don't do well with pain..."
"Keiichi-san, you just need to stand quietly and follow my lead! Rika and I will handle everything! Honestly! To think I have to look after him during the punishment game too... Honestly!"
...I already knew that her sullen manner was the outward sign of her being in genuinely high spirits.
So it made me want to smile.
I turned to Satoko and said...

"Say.
Would you make it again sometime?"

"The bento.
...It was wonderful."

"If you think that bento reflects my true skill, I am deeply insulted.
Next time I will make something far more impressive for you!"
"I am going to get far, far better. And next time I will make you groan in speechless admiration, and claim an unanimous, undisputed victory——"
I stroked Satoko's head roughly before she could finish.

"Last night's supper was wonderful too.
Anything you make—it's all going to be delicious."
"...............I-I shall pass that along directly to the shop where I bought the side dishes and the tinned food."
She said it sharply and briskly walked away.

"Not being able to accept a compliment gracefully is one of Satoko's endearing qualities.
Nipah~☆"
"Rika, you need not add such unnecessary remarks!"
"Mii~☆"
Chatting like that as we walked, we found ourselves at the door to the teacher's lounge.
"Now then! The punishment game is nothing to be afraid of if we face it together! Let's go!"
Slaaaam!!!
We opened the door to the land of the dead!!
Chie-sensei was, as she was without fail every day—as reliably as the sun rising in the eastern sky—
...eating a curry bento today as well.
It was store-bought retort pouch curry, but she made a point of choosing a different brand every day.
changeReason: Adds 'retort pouch' specifying the type of store-bought curry, matching レトルト in the Japanese

TextENGNew:
"Oh my.
Maebara-kun, Furude-san, and Houjou-san.
...Is something the matter?"
She appeared to be in the midst of a moment of supreme bliss, her cheeks full of her beloved curry.
...And we were here to start a fight with her—what a terrible thing this punishment game was.
But we had to do it!
Because this... was the rule of our club!!

"...All right, here we go.
Ready?"

"Keiichi-san, please do not take charge here.
Leave this entirely to us!"
I-I see...
"...Do your best, yaaay."
Rika-chan smiled her little "nipah~☆".
All right, my resolve was set. I would follow these two's lead! Satoko, Rika-chan...... my life was in their hands!

Satoko and Rika-chan drew in a slow, deep breath.
...And then...
"H-Hey, you! You smug curry devotee—you're single because you reek of garlic~~~~!!!!"

Chie-sensei's eyes shifted with terrifying swiftness into something cold and absolute. Somehow, even the room temperature of the teacher's lounge seemed to plummet below freezing..........W-Wait! It actually dropped?! It really did?!
Satoko and Rika-chan had already vanished from before me.
......They had disappeared?
"——Maebara-kun. Listen carefully."
Chie-sensei rose with slow, almost slow-motion deliberateness—and then in a single soundless instant was directly before me, her face mere centimeters from mine.
"T-That, um, I, the thing is...!?"

"You have committed three errors.
The first—speaking ill of curry.
The second—speaking ill of curry.
And the third——
speaking ill of curry!!!"
Isn't that only one error?!
——Is what I thought, but there was no possible way I could say that aloud.
"Allow me to share with you a very fine saying——"
"A-And what might that be?"
"Spilled curry can never be put back."
......What happened after that—I would prefer not to remember. Actually, please don't ask.
When I returned to the classroom after my deeply enriching experience, everyone welcomed me back with great warmth.
"Oh~, good work! I'm amazed you made it back~?"
"Your reaction was far too slow, Keiichi-san! Hit-and-run is the most basic of basic tactics!!"
"...Poor Keiichi, left behind all on his own."

Satoko was already in the middle of her usual triumphant laughter, while Rika-chan was patting me on the head—whether in comfort or in mockery, I could not tell. I hauled my collapsed body upright and cast a withering glare at the two of them who had fled.
"I-If you were going to run, tell me in advance...! Am I just a sacrificial pawn to you two?!"
"What are you talking about?! How many times did I tell you to leave everything to us?!"

"If you had been watching the two of us carefully, you would have known exactly when to run.
——Your mistake was watching Chie instead of watching us."
That's easy for you to say... Isn't it only natural to watch the face of the person standing right in front of you?!

"People tense up when they look someone in the face or eyes.
If you want to find an opening, you need to read their whole body and anticipate their movements."
"Rika-chan... you're describing how to be a master swordsman...?"

"I'm glad you were able to learn so much.
Now Keiichi will be perfectly fine next time.
Nipah~☆"

Rika-chan gave me a pleasant smile.
...Which was lovely, but there wasn't going to be a next time—I never wanted to go through that again.

"...Speaking of which, I heard, Kei-chan.
Turns out you ended up getting help from Satoko and the others with lunch, did you?"
Mion grinned and gave a dramatic shrug. It seemed Satoko and Rika-chan had filled her in on the whole story when they got back.
"Well, I could have simply left him to it! I merely let him understand who he was dealing with!"
"...Had it not been for us, the Maebara estate would have been converted into a charcoal-firing kiln by now."
changeReason: Corrects 'mansion of Ashes' to 'charcoal-firing kiln' matching 炭焼き小屋

TextENGNew: I-I asked you so many times to keep that a secret...!
"Ahahahahaha! You really are something, Keiichi! You really are! You're a natural entertainer!!"

"...Slapstick entertainment is something you watch other people do.
As it turns out, doing it yourself is absolutely no fun at all!!"

"Well, setting that aside...
So?
Your mother won't be home again today, will she?
How about this old man lends a hand?
Hm?"

"Huh, really? That would be a huge h——
Oh."
For just a moment, I had been about to express delight.
But then I looked past Mion and met Satoko's eyes... and my mouth instinctively closed.
Satoko's expression was indescribably complicated.

Satoko surely still remembered the offhand comment I had made about wanting to eat her cooking again. It was possible that she had been quietly prepared to come and make dinner for me again today as well......
In that case, today——
"——What a wonderful idea! The chance to savor Mion-san's cooking might be a once-in-a-lifetime blessing for you, Keiichi-san~~?"
Before I could decline Mion's offer, Satoko pushed it forward with a smile. Then she turned to me, her face spread wide with a teasing grin.
"Keiichi-san, please accept graciously and with gratitude! You were planning on eating that saved-up cup ramen today anyway, weren't you? There is no comparison~?"
changeReason: Corrects 'instant noodles' to 'saved-up cup ramen' matching とっておきのカップラーメン

TextENGNew: There is no comparison.
She was surely referring to her own cooking as well.
But I found it so delicious. There was no need for her to put herself down like that...
"All right, then it's settled! I'll go do some shopping and then come over to your place, so just wait——"

"Hm?
What is it, Rena?"

"...Hau~, Mii-chan.
Why not take a look at all 360 degrees of the world around you~"

"...Would someone please teach Mii how to read the room.
Rawr~"

"R-Rawr...?
Read the room... what does that mean?"
"...It means—go ahead and score your point already."

"Hey.
I want to eat some stir-fried vegetables... Satoko, can you make that?"
"W-Well, there is nothing I cannot do if I put my mind to it!"

"...She makes it very well.
I love Satoko's stir-fry."
She had looked uncertain at first, but once she received Rika-chan's seal of approval, Satoko turned back to me with her confidence restored.

"It's settled, then.
Let's go shopping for things after school today.
I don't really know what to buy, after all."

"That is true.
Shopping at the supermarket is still a little early for you, Keiichi-san! It makes me anxious if I am not keeping an eye on you!"
"...And that Satoko also makes me anxious that she will buy the wrong things if I am not watching her."
"R-Rikaaaa!!"
"Meep~~!!"
Chatting like that as we walked, we found ourselves at the door to the teacher's lounge.
"Now then! Let's get the punishment game over with quickly, then we can talk about tonight's menu!"
"...Meep☆"

Rika-chan grabbed onto my arm with a look of delight.
So in turn I reached my other hand to pull Satoko close by the shoulder.

"We all go together in death, alright?
I won't let anyone die alone!"

"...We are all together.
I won't let anyone die alone."
"T-That is an indiscreet thing to say! I want no part of anything so alarming!!"

She grumbled, but...
She was so happy about it that even I was getting embarrassed.

"All right.
Beyond this single door lies the world after death! But we will return!
All of us will!!"
"Yes!! All of us!! Now... let us go forth!!"
Slaaaam!!!
We opened the door to the land of the dead!!
Chie-sensei was, as she was without fail every day—as reliably as the sun rising in the eastern sky—
...eating a curry bento today as well.
It was store-bought retort pouch curry, but she made a point of choosing a different brand every day.
changeReason: Adds 'retort pouch' specifying the type of store-bought curry, matching レトルト in the Japanese

TextENGNew:
"Oh my.
Maebara-kun, Furude-san, and Houjou-san.
...Is something the matter?"
She appeared to be in the midst of a moment of supreme bliss, her cheeks full of her beloved curry.
...And we were here to start a fight with her—what a terrible thing this punishment game was.
But we had to do it!
Because this... was the rule of our club!!
The three of us clasped each other's shoulders tightly once more.

"...All right, here we go.
Ready?"
"Keiichi-san, you are to stay quietly in place!! Rika and I will handle everything!! Entrust your life to me for now!"
"...Do your best, yaaay."

"...What are you three doing?
......???"
Two or three question marks could be seen floating above the teacher's head.
All right, Satoko.

I was entrusting my life to you, but just this once.
...Because I would be keeping my eyes firmly shut and thinking only of what we would have for dinner tonight...!!
I was counting on her...!!!

Satoko drew in a slow, deep breath.
...And then...
"H-Hey, you! You smug curry devotee—you're single because you reek of garlic~~~~!!!!"
